channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Microsoft's npm Packages Got Backdoored. Again. And AI Agents Pulled the Trigger.
item.description: 73 cryptographically signed npm packages from Microsoft were compromised last week with advanced credential-stealing malware that fires the moment a developer opens one in an AI coding agent....

item.title: Give Your AI Assistant Infrastructure Eyes Before It Writes Another Query
item.description: You asked Claude Code to add pagination to your order history endpoint. It generated a clean function — listOrdersByUser() — using a DynamoDB Scan with a Limit parameter. It compiled. Tests...

item.title: OSINT Prompts for ChatGPT and Claude: 5 Templates and a Repeatable Method
item.description: Most people using AI for OSINT are doing it backwards. They open ChatGPT or Claude, type "find everything on this email," and hope. The model produces something that looks confident, reads well,...

item.title: L'IA ne remplace pas les développeurs — elle dissout ce qui les unissait
item.description: Lundi matin, Libreville. Ndong ouvre le dépôt avant même son café. Le sprint prévoyait qu'il code trois endpoints cette semaine — le backend, son métier depuis sept ans. Ils sont déjà là. Commités...

item.title: June 17 - Build Vision Data Agents with Tools, Skills, and MCP
item.description: Join us on June 17 for a virtual workshop to learn how to build production-ready AI agents. Register for the Zoom! Learn how to build production-ready AI agents that can reason over your data,...

item.title: I built an AI thing for Amazon sellers, and honestly it works way better than I expected
item.description: So here's the thing. If you sell on Amazon, you already know this pain: good product photos cost a stupid amount of money. Like, a single lifestyle shot from a real photographer? That's $50 to...

item.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more
item.description: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its longstanding Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

item.title: Decoupling Microservices with Event-Driven SQS Pipelines for High-Volume Fulfillment
item.description: When scaling an enterprise backend, a massive architectural trap is tight coupling. If your order placement service directly calls your inventory allocation service via a synchronous HTTP request,...

item.title: Angular 22: O Fim do Boilerplate e a Consolidação da Era Reativa
item.description: Se você acompanha a evolução do framework do Google nos últimos anos, sabe que ele vem passando por uma reconstrução silenciosa — peça por peça. Com o lançamento do Angular 22 em 3 de junho de...

item.title: Developer Documentation Platforms in 2026: GitBook, Mintlify, ReadMe, Docusaurus, and Docsbook Compared
item.description: Which platform actually fits how your team works — and how AI agents are starting to consume your docs? There's a weird thing that happens at a lot of startups. Someone sets up a shiny...

item.title: Claude Fable 5: What It Is and What It Means for Developers
item.description: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 today, June 9, 2026. It is the most capable model Anthropic has ever made generally available, and it lands at a price point below half of what Claude Mythos...

item.title: Fitbit’s Charge 6 and Ace LTE are now as cheap as the new $100 Air
item.description: Whether you’re shopping for Father’s Day or trying to keep your kids entertained over summer break, you don’t need to spend a fortune to get a great Fitbit right now. You can currently pick up the...

item.title: Apple wants Europe to blink
item.description: It took a few years, but Apple finally made its AI look useful. Now millions of iPhone users in Europe are being told they won't be getting Siri AI anytime soon, if ever - and Apple wants them to...

item.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
item.description: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond...

item.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Upgrade for Cyber Partners and a ‘Safe’ Version for the Rest of You
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

item.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
item.description: Anthropic just announced Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available. According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in...

item.title: SwitchBot’s E Ink Weather Station is already 20 percent off
item.description: We’re only a few days since the SwitchBot Smart E Ink Weather Station launched, and you can already save $25 on the smart home gadget. Visit Amazon and clip the on-page coupon to bring the price...

item.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
item.description: Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries...

item.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
item.description: Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject...

item.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
item.description: Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should...

item.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
item.description: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello...

item.title: High-severity vulnerability in Linux caused by a single errant character
item.description: Use-after-free bug can be exploited to evade sandbox defenses.

item.title: GPS As a Key Distribution Platform
item.description: This is interesting: The U.S. military has likely been quietly broadcasting codes for its global encryption network using public GPS for nearly 20 years, turning each satellite into a hidden...

item.title: Square Enix teases Kingdom Hearts 4 at Nintendo Direct
item.description: A different kind of Sora is back in the news. Square Enix just teased the long-awaited next entry in the Kingdom Hearts series, though there's not much in the way of info just yet. It's called...

item.title: Meta will use your activity on other websites to personalize your feeds
item.description: Meta is planning to use the data shared by other businesses to personalize your feed and its AI responses. In a blog post on Tuesday, Meta explains that it already uses your off-platform activity,...

item.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time is getting a remake for the Switch 2
item.description: Nintendo announced a remake of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time during its Nintendo Direct presentation on Tuesday. It will launch sometime in 2026 on Nintendo Switch 2. More details will be...

item.title: Microsoft AI chief walks back comments about AI taking over white-collar work
item.description: Microsoft AI head Mustafa Suleyman is walking back his statement about AI automating jobs done by white-collar workers, including lawyers, accountants, and project managers. During an episode of...

item.title: Sandstone raises $30M to bring AI to in-house legal teams
item.description: Sandstone's Series A comes just six months after a Sequoia-led seed round.

item.title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
item.description: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.
